headline: Software Engineer | Conversational AI backend, Python APIs, LLM-powered production systems on AWS
disciplineOtherNote: Backend engineer with strong focus on conversational AI platforms, LLM integrations, and production API reliability.
targetRolesNarrative: I am targeting backend software engineer and AI platform roles building conversational AI systems, backend services, APIs, and cloud infrastructure for production LLM applications. I enjoy work on automated human-like conversations across email, SMS, and chat, scalable data pipelines, and integrations with internal systems and external data sources.
companiesYouAdmire: Teams building practical AI applications in production, especially conversational AI and developer-focused infrastructure companies.
honestCareerNarrative: I started in backend services at an enterprise integration shop, then moved into a conversational AI startup where I built Python backend services powering customer engagement workflows across email, SMS, and chat. My recent work centers on production LLM integrations, API design, AWS infrastructure, PostgreSQL data models, and reliability improvements for high-volume messaging pipelines. I collaborate closely with product managers and data scientists to ship features safely in real production environments.
proudestProfessionalWins:
Conversational AI platform scale: Built and improved backend services for a conversational AI platform handling automated customer conversations and lead qualification, improving throughput and reliability across email, SMS, and chat channels.

LLM production integration: Partnered with data scientists to integrate large language models into production APIs with guardrails, monitoring, and fallback behavior for enterprise customers.

Infrastructure reliability: Designed scalable cloud infrastructure and data pipelines on AWS with PostgreSQL, reducing incident frequency during peak campaign traffic.
gapsOrNonTraditionalPath: I completed a backend-focused bootcamp module on distributed systems before my first production API role, which accelerated my move into platform-style backend work.
resumePlainText:
Maya Chen
Software Engineer — Conversational AI Backend
Chicago, IL | United States, Remote

SUMMARY
Backend software engineer with 6+ years building Python backend services, APIs, and AWS infrastructure for conversational AI and LLM-powered production systems.

EXPERIENCE
Software Engineer — DialogFlow Systems — Remote | 2021 – Present
• Build and improve backend services that power a conversational AI platform for automated human-like conversations across email, SMS, and chat.
• Develop APIs and integrations with internal systems and external data sources for customer engagement workflows.
• Work with modern AI technologies and large language models (LLMs) in production environments.
• Design and implement scalable cloud infrastructure and data pipelines on AWS with PostgreSQL.
• Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and data scientists to ship new platform features.
• Contribute to system reliability, performance improvements, and monitoring for high-traffic messaging services.

Backend Engineer — Northwind Integrations — Chicago, IL | 2018 – 2021
• Developed Python backend web frameworks and REST APIs for customer data integrations.
• Built distributed data systems and background workers for event processing.
• Implemented observability, alerting, and performance tuning for production services.

PROJECTS
Conversation Gateway — Python, AWS, PostgreSQL, LLM APIs
Backend services and APIs supporting multi-channel conversational workflows with monitoring and retry semantics.

Campaign Insights Pipeline — Python, AWS, PostgreSQL
Data pipeline for conversation analytics and operational reporting used by customer success teams.

EDUCATION
BS Computer Science — University of Illinois Chicago — 2018

TECH STACK
Python, backend web frameworks, REST APIs, AWS cloud infrastructure, PostgreSQL, distributed data systems, LLMs, Docker, GitHub Actions, monitoring/observability
skillsCoreNarrative: Core strengths include Python backend development, REST APIs, conversational AI platform services, production LLM integration, AWS infrastructure, PostgreSQL, distributed data systems, data pipelines, and system reliability. I enjoy building practical AI applications with strong monitoring, performance tuning, and cross-functional collaboration with product and data science teams.
toolsOtherNote: Python, backend web frameworks, REST APIs, LLM tooling, SMS/chat/email channel integrations, PostgreSQL tuning, AWS ECS/Lambda/RDS.
educationDetails: BS Computer Science, University of Illinois Chicago (2018). Coursework in distributed systems, databases, and software engineering.

projects.title: Conversation Gateway
projects.summary: Python backend services and APIs supporting multi-channel conversational workflows across email, SMS, and chat. Includes LLM orchestration hooks, PostgreSQL persistence, AWS deployment, and operational dashboards for message delivery and conversation state.
projects.techStackExtra: Python, AWS, PostgreSQL, LLM APIs, REST, monitoring.
projects.impactMetrics: Used as a reference implementation for channel-agnostic conversational backend design.

projects.title: Campaign Insights Pipeline
projects.summary: Distributed data pipeline ingesting conversation events into PostgreSQL and analytics stores for customer success and product teams. Built with Python workers, AWS infrastructure, and reliability-focused retry and dead-letter handling.
projects.techStackExtra: Python, AWS, PostgreSQL, data pipelines, observability.
projects.impactMetrics: Reduced time to diagnose conversation funnel issues during live campaigns.

storyHardestTechnicalChallenge: We hit unpredictable latency spikes when LLM-backed conversation steps shared a single worker pool with legacy rule-based flows. I separated workloads, added queue isolation, and introduced adaptive timeouts and fallback responses so customer-facing conversations remained stable during model provider outages.
storyDisagreementOrConflict: Product wanted to expose raw model outputs directly to end users for speed. I documented safety and quality risks and proposed a staged rollout with human-review thresholds, which the team adopted and later credited with avoiding a major customer escalation.
storyBiggestMistake: I under-scoped database migration testing for a conversation state schema change and caused a brief replay issue. I added mandatory shadow validation and rollback drills afterward.
storyLeadingWithoutAuthority: Coordinated backend, data science, and support teams to define a shared incident playbook for conversational AI outages, improving mean time to recovery across channels.
storyTightDeadline: Delivered a critical SMS channel integration ahead of a product launch by scoping to core conversation states first and shipping follow-up analytics in a second release.
storyConflictingPriorities: Balanced a production LLM regression investigation with a committed API migration by negotiating a short extension and parallelizing test coverage with a peer engineer.
storyProcessImprovement: Introduced conversation trace IDs across services and dashboards, making cross-service debugging much faster for on-call engineers.
storyDifficultFeedback: Was told my design docs were too implementation-heavy for stakeholders. I added concise problem statements and outcome metrics at the top while keeping technical appendices for engineers.
storyMentoringTeaching: Mentored a junior engineer on Python API design and production debugging for messaging workflows, helping them lead a small feature end-to-end.
storyCrossFunctionalCollaboration: Worked with product managers and data scientists to define safe LLM prompt boundaries and evaluation metrics tied to business outcomes.
storyAmbiguousProblem: Customer reports of missing conversation replies had no obvious owner across channel adapters, workers, and model services. I mapped the full path and isolated a race in state persistence.
storyEthicalOrRiskTradeoff: Pushed back on logging full message content in production traces without retention controls, implementing redaction and tighter access policies instead.
whatYouWantNextNote: Want backend AI platform roles building conversational systems and production LLM applications with strong engineering culture and operational maturity.
compensationExtraNote: Open to discussing equity at the right stage; prioritize interesting AI platform problems and healthy on-call practices.
workAuthOtherNote: US citizen authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.
